URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: Independent open-source project; primary developer: Julio Jerez; co-developer: Alain Suero (original co-author); community contributions via GitHub

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: High-performance physics simulation engine for games and interactive applications; provides rigid body dynamics, collision detection, soft bodies, vehicle dynamics, and articulated rigid bodies; optimized for real-time deterministic simulation with exact arithmetic; provides Newtonian dynamics solver with an exact implicit solver for rigid body integrations; widely used in game development and robotics simulation

How is the project funded? (unfunded, unclear, funded∗) where ∗ requires a string to say the source of funding

Last commit date? (date)
Status? (alive is defined as presence of commits in the last 18 months) (alive, dead, unclear)
License? (GNU GPL, BSD, MIT, terms of use, trial, none, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via a string
Platforms? (set of Windows, Linux, OS X, Android, other∗) ∗ given via string

Software Category? The concept category includes software that does not have an officially released version. Public software has a released version in the public domain. Private software has a released version available to authorized users only. (concept, public, private)
Development model? (open source, freeware, commercial, unclear)
Publications about the software? Refers to publications that have used or mentioned the software. (number or unknown)
Source code URL? (set of url, n/a, unclear)
Programming language(s)? (set of FORTRAN, Matlab, C, C++, Java, R, Ruby, Python, Cython, BASIC, Pascal, IDL, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via string
Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Newton Dynamics is notable for its deterministic exact arithmetic solver, which distinguishes it from other game physics engines like Bullet and PhysX. The v4.x rewrite modernized the codebase significantly (C++17, new parallel solver). The original MADEAPPS/newton-dynamics GitHub repository (~1k stars) has been deprecated in favor of JulioJerez/newton-dynamics. Integrated in Gazebo as an optional physics engine. Used in commercial games and robotics simulations. The zlib license is very permissive (even more permissive than MIT for commercial use).

Response: Standard CMake build for a physics library. No prebuilt binaries for Linux (Windows has prebuilt DLLs). Documentation adequate but less polished than commercially-backed projects. 6-step source build is typical for C++ physics libraries.

Response: Physics correctness implicitly validated through use in commercial games and robotics. Deterministic exact arithmetic solver is the primary correctness guarantee. Absence of CI is a weakness for tracking regressions.

Response: 20+ years of commercial game use provides industry-level reliability validation. Core rigid body simulation is very stable. v4.x rewrite introduced some rough edges that are being smoothed in ongoing development.

Response: Error handling functional but less comprehensive than professionally-maintained scientific libraries. Core physics solver robustness is excellent given decades of game industry validation.

Response: Single primary maintainer creates sustainability risk. The v4.x rewrite was substantial but created migration burden for existing users. Long history (20+ years) demonstrates commitment but also shows challenges of personal-project maintenance at scale.

Metric: Is API documented? (yes, no, n/a)
Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Zlib license is very permissive (even for closed-source commercial use). C API with C++ wrapper enables integration in any language via FFI. Python and Lua bindings available. Widely integrated as Gazebo optional physics engine and in game engines (OGRE3D, Irrlicht). API stability within major version maintained.
